intro: Leonhardia lurida, also known as Leonhardia, is a genus of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae, consisting of a single species that is native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Leonhardia lurida include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
